abbreviations.definition: invisible decrease (like sc2tog, but only in the front loops)

rounds.title: Rnd 1
rounds.rawInstruction: Create a magic ring with 6 sc in the ring. Close the ring.
rounds.summary: Create a magic ring with 6 single crochets and close the ring.

rounds.title: Rnd 2
rounds.summary: Increase in every stitch around, six times.

rounds.title: Rnd 3
rounds.summary: Work 2 sc, then 2 increases, place marker; repeat sequence 3 times.

rounds.title: Rnd 4
rounds.rawInstruction: (4 sc, 2 inc) x 3 (24 sc). Move Markers up as you go.
rounds.summary: Work 4 sc, then 2 increases; repeat 3 times, moving markers up.

rounds.title: Rnd 5
rounds.summary: Work 7 sc, then increase; repeat 3 times.

rounds.title: Rnd 6
rounds.rawInstruction: (Work to 1 sc before marker and inc x 2) x 3 (33 sc)
rounds.summary: Work to 1 sc before each marker, then work 2 increases; repeat 3 times.

rounds.title: Rnd 7
rounds.rawInstruction: (Work to marker and inc in marked stitch) x 3 (36 sc)
rounds.summary: Work to each marker and increase in the marked stitch; repeat 3 times.

rounds.title: Sew in magic ring tail
rounds.rawInstruction: Using the tapestry needle, sew in the magic ring's 6" tail.
rounds.summary: Weave in the tail from the magic ring.

rounds.title: Repeat Rnds 6 & 7 for cup size
rounds.rawInstruction: Select the cup size and continue. A cup – Repeat Rnds 6 & 7 three times until 4.5" across or 13 rounds (approx. 63 sc). B cup – Repeat Rnds 6 & 7 four times until 5" across or 15 rounds (approx.72 sc). C cup – Repeat Rnds 6 & 7 five times until 5.5" across or 17 rounds (approx. 81 sc). D cup – Repeat Rnds 6 & 7 six times until 6" across or 19 rounds (approx 90 sc). DD cup – Repeat Rnds 6 & 7 seven times until 6.5" across or 21 rounds (approx. 99 sc).
rounds.summary: Repeat Rnds 6 and 7 according to cup size to achieve desired diameter.

rounds.title: Turning Rows 1-2
rounds.rawInstruction: Work even through the back loop only.
rounds.summary: Work two rounds even, crocheting through the back loop only.

rounds.title: Position before first marker
rounds.rawInstruction: When the size is correct, the turning rows have been completed, and you're ready to start decreasing, crochet until 1 sc before the first marker.
rounds.summary: Crochet to 1 sc before the first marker to prepare for decreases.

rounds.title: Rnd 1
rounds.rawInstruction: (inv dec (PM), inv dec, then sc until 1 sc before the next marker) x 3.
rounds.summary: Work invisible decrease, place marker, another invisible decrease, then sc to 1 before next marker; repeat 3 times.

rounds.title: Repeat Rnd 1
rounds.rawInstruction: Repeat Rnd 1 until you have 15 sc left.
rounds.summary: Repeat the decrease round until only 15 sc remain.

rounds.title: Cut yarn
rounds.summary: Cut the working yarn, leaving a 10-inch tail for finishing.

rounds.title: Weave through front loops
rounds.rawInstruction: Thread tapestry needle and weave through the front loop only for the remaining 15 sc.
rounds.summary: Thread needle and weave through front loops of remaining 15 stitches.

rounds.title: Stuff or leave open
rounds.rawInstruction: If stuffing: slightly overstuff with Poly-fil, do not close hole, tuck tail into Knocker. If mailing unstuffed: leave hole open and yarn tail outside.
rounds.summary: Either stuff the Knocker or leave open for mailing, depending on distribution method.

materials: 1 (1, 1, 2, 2) skeins of yarn from the Knitted Knocker approved yarn list
3 locking stitch markers (1 in a different color for start of round)
